---
title: "Project Phase 2- Preliminary Analysis"
author: "Team Popcorn - Amritha, Ahana, Keerthana"
date: "05/3/2026"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
    highlight: tango
    latex_engine: xelatex
  
---
# So we have three tasks in the MST_Data folder 
├── Both_item_task/
│   └── both_data/                # Data files for Experiment 1
├── item_only/
│   └── item_only_data/           # Data files for Experiment 2
├── task_only/
│   └── task_only_data/           # Data files for Experiment 3

# Dataset Description

The dataset used in this study is organized into three experimental conditions of the Mnemonic Similarity Task (MST): **Both Item + Task**, **Item-only**, and **Task-only**. Each condition is stored in a separate directory within the `MST_Data` folder.

Within each condition, the data is structured at the participant level. For every participant, there are two CSV files corresponding to the two phases of the experiment:

* a **task file**, representing the encoding (study) phase
* a **test file**, representing the retrieval (memory test) phase

The files follow a consistent naming convention of the form:
`<subjectID>_MST_<phase>_<date>_<timestamp>.csv`,
where the subject ID uniquely identifies each participant and the phase indicates whether the file corresponds to the encoding or test stage.

The task files primarily contain information related to stimulus presentation and encoding responses, including image paths, trial indices, and key responses recorded during the study phase.

The test files contain trial-level behavioral data used for analysis, including:

stimulus identity (e.g., scene or object images)
stimulus condition (e.g., target, lure, foil — inferred from stimulus position/type)
participant responses (e.g., old, similar, new)
reaction times associated with each response

From these test-phase variables, key behavioral measures are computed:

LDI (Lure Discrimination Index), reflecting the ability to distinguish similar items
REC (Recognition Score), reflecting general recognition performance
Reaction Time (RT), capturing response latency

The dataset is structured such that each participant contributes multiple trials, enabling within-subject analysis of memory performance across different stimulus types.
